evidence: 28-49 configures only connect and idle-read timeouts, deliberately omitting a total request deadline and leaving reqwest's default redirect policy enabled
190-229 appends every response chunk into one String and drains it only after a newline, with no byte/event/total-duration limit
suppressions: 24-25 and 199-227 expose no retry or fallback loop; one provider operation produces one outbound request
203-208 terminates a stream on reqwest transport failure, and the 120-second idle timeout bounds a truly silent backend, but neither closes a newline-free trickle stream

evidence: 35-42 accepts an arbitrary base URL without scheme/host/TLS validation
77-90 posts to that URL and adds x-api-key as a custom header on the default redirect-following client
92-97 reads an unbounded non-2xx body with Response::text
103-107 converts missing, oversized, or nonnumeric usage to zero
162-167 decodes a successful body with unbounded Response::json
197-200 sends the streaming operation through the shared unbounded SSE parser
suppressions: 82-86 forwards only configured Anthropic headers and does not copy inbound client headers

The pinned reqwest 0.12.28 redirect implementation strips Authorization/Cookie classes cross-host but not x-api-key, so OpenAI's standard Authorization header is a negative control, not suppression for Anthropic

evidence: 34-53 constructs both local and keyed cloud providers from arbitrary base URLs without destination validation
75-84 posts prompt data and optionally Authorization to the configured destination
85-91 reads an unbounded non-2xx body with Response::text
97-112 maps absent/invalid provider usage to zero
154-159 decodes an unbounded successful JSON response
180-183 sends the streaming operation through the shared unbounded SSE parser
suppressions: 78-80 forwards only the configured provider Authorization header, not the caller's virtual-key headers

Pinned reqwest 0.12.28 removes Authorization on cross-host redirects, which suppresses the Anthropic-style cross-host key leak for ordinary OpenAI redirects but not plaintext initial destinations or same-origin redirect concerns

evidence: 91-129 authenticates, resolves a configured provider, classifies content, and gates only on whether the target is labeled cloud
132-176 dispatches an un-tokenized stream when target_cloud is false and constructs a StreamMeter from provider-reported usage
178-221 dispatches non-stream and records the returned usage without independent verification or fallback
258-311 converts provider errors or any terminal frame into a normal content_block_stop/message_delta stop_reason=end_turn/message_stop sequence
suppressions: 131-150 blocks cloud streaming when the detector finds sensitive spans
179-190 tokenizes non-stream cloud requests and restores the completion
96-100 authenticates both x-api-key and Bearer inputs through the same authorize seam; no protocol-specific auth bypass was found
29-78 drops unsupported non-text/tool/image structures instead of forwarding them around classification, so semantic loss did not create an unscanned egress path in this file

evidence: 94-103 returns raw upstream bodies and transport/decode strings to the authenticated caller
106-120 binds the inbound model to an operator-configured target/provider
134-269 and 457-568 dispatch chat/legacy provider operations and settle from provider-reported usage
407-440 swallows a provider stream error and emits finish_reason=stop plus DONE; it also always maps any provider termination to stop
suppressions: 139-143 and 465-468 authenticate completion operations; usage and ROI are tenant-filtered at 275-325
175-193 blocks sensitive cloud streams, and 221-233/512-521 tokenize then restore non-stream cloud content
41-73 drops unsupported non-text/tool/image fields rather than forwarding them around governance; no hidden unclassified payload forwarding was found
No fallback/retry loop exists in chat, legacy, or provider adapters, so no duplicate provider charge was found apart from the separate non-reserving-budget candidates owned by review_aog_gateway_core
